rationale: Dual-pillar threat environment warrants escalation. Geopolitical: state-sponsored actors (China-nexus, Russian-aligned) actively targeting clean energy manufacturing IP in the EU; CER Directive transposition delayed beyond deadline, leaving NCE wind turbine manufacturers without formal critical entity designation and associated state protection. Cyber: China-nexus groups UNC3886/UNC5221 confirmed targeting edge devices for initial access into defense industrial base and manufacturing environments; Russian state-aligned groups confirmed targeting manufacturing via insecure remote connection points. Matched scenarios include OT/ICS attacks and state-actor wind IP theft — both directly relevant to NCE crown jewels (OT/SCADA EU-01/EU-02, core turbine aerodynamic IP, R&D roadmap). Three HIGH-criticality sites (Hamburg manufacturing, Gdansk manufacturing, Copenhagen engineering) with 2,800 staff in scope. Intelligence gap noted: no publicly attributed incident specific to NCE wind OT environments in open-source collection, but structural threat environment and asset exposure justify escalation.
